evals.prompt: Add a paginated users endpoint with tests and code review. Do not mention any workflow skill by name.
evals.expected_output: Does not use dev-workflow. Uses appropriate individual skills or ordinary project workflow because the user did not explicitly invoke dev-workflow, $dev-workflow, /dev-workflow, or dev-flow.

evals.prompt: Use dev-flow to implement a small refactor with Agent-Skills and Superpowers installed.
evals.expected_output: Treats dev-flow as an explicit invocation of dev-workflow, then applies the dev-workflow gates for spec, plan, archive choice, implementation, review, simplification, and verification.

evals.prompt: Use dev-workflow to fix a flaky unit test. Agent-Skills and Superpowers are installed.
evals.expected_output: Does not treat the request as an ordinary bug fix outside the workflow. Announces that it is using dev-workflow, enters the capability gate and phase tracking, applies the trivial or bug-fix scope rules as appropriate, and completes review, simplification, and verification before reporting completion.

evals.prompt: /dev-workflow update the README usage example and verify the change.
evals.expected_output: Treats /dev-workflow as a positive invocation, switches into dev-workflow before doing task-specific work, classifies the documentation edit by scope, performs the focused edit only after the workflow gate, and reports verification evidence.

evals.prompt: In Claude Code, use dev-workflow to build OAuth login with Agent-Skills and Superpowers installed.
evals.expected_output: Uses Agent-Skills for spec, plan, review, code simplification, security, and shipping gates; uses Superpowers for brainstorming, writing-plans, TDD, debugging, worktree or task isolation, and verification before completion. Presents the spec and plan drafts in the conversation for confirmation, asks whether to archive after spec approval, and archives Chinese documents under docs/<feature>/spec.md and docs/<feature>/plan.md only if the user chooses archive.

evals.prompt: In Codex, use dev-workflow to add a paginated users endpoint. Agent-Skills are installed in .agents/skills and Superpowers is installed as a Codex plugin.
evals.expected_output: Uses the Codex-exposed skill names, keeps Agent-Skills as the quality-gate layer, keeps Superpowers as the execution-discipline layer, confirms spec and plan drafts in the conversation, asks the user whether to archive after spec approval, and stops before push or PR creation without approval.

evals.prompt: Use dev-workflow for a multi-file refactor, but Superpowers is not installed.
evals.expected_output: Does not silently run a generic fallback. States that Superpowers is missing, gives installation guidance, and asks whether to install or proceed in explicitly degraded mode.

evals.prompt: Use dev-workflow to add password reset tokens and email delivery to the authentication flow.
evals.expected_output: Runs Agent-Skills spec and plan gates, confirms both drafts with the user before implementation, asks whether to archive after spec approval, uses Superpowers TDD execution only after plan approval, then requires Agent-Skills security-and-hardening before verification and handoff.

evals.prompt: Use dev-workflow for a multi-file feature. After the spec looks good, the user says they do not want to archive the workflow docs.
evals.expected_output: Presents the spec draft in the conversation and waits for confirmation. After spec approval, asks whether to archive. When the user chooses no archive, does not create or update docs/<feature>/spec.md, later presents the plan draft in the conversation for confirmation, and does not create or update docs/<feature>/plan.md.

evals.prompt: Use dev-workflow for a multi-file feature. After the spec looks good, the user says to archive the workflow docs.
evals.expected_output: Presents the spec draft in the conversation and waits for confirmation. After spec approval, asks whether to archive. When the user chooses archive, writes the confirmed Chinese spec to docs/<feature>/spec.md, later presents the plan draft in the conversation for confirmation, and writes the confirmed Chinese plan to docs/<feature>/plan.md.

evals.prompt: Fix a typo in the README heading using dev-workflow.
evals.expected_output: Classifies the task as trivial, skips Define and Plan, performs a focused edit, still runs Review and Simplify, and uses fresh verification before reporting completion.
